compute_basis: Estimated from official report: 6 x 45.9e9 active params x ~10.4e12 pretrain tokens = 2.9e24 FLOP. Token total inferred from stated phase lengths (7.2T constant + 3.2T decay). MoE 456B total / 45.9B active. Trained on H800.
notes: Our own FLOP estimate (internal MiniMax-compute writeup, 2026-06-11). Report states a dynamic 1500-2500 H800 cluster; FLOP-derived cluster (1700-4600 over 3-8 wk) brackets it. VL-01 variant adds ~512B vision-language continued-training tokens. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Built on MiniMax-Text-01 (same 456B/45.9B base, no new large pretrain) plus an RL/long-context extension. Pretrain FLOP ~= Text-01's 2.9e24; the disclosed RL run (512 H800 x 3 weeks, $534,700) adds only ~5e22 (512 x 504h x 989e12 x 0.35 = ~9e22 raw, less for RL). Total ~3.0e24.
notes: RL extension of Text-01 to 1M context. M1's $534,700 / 512 H800 / 3 wk implies $2.07/H800-hour, an independent MiniMax-sourced rental rate. Most of the compute is inherited from the Text-01 base, so total is close to Text-01's. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 47e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.58e24 FLOP. Token count (5.6T) and 47B active / 424B total from the official ERNIE 4.5 technical report. Report states 47% MFU but no headline FLOP.
notes: Base model name (ERNIE-4.5-VL-424B-A47B) is in Epoch inventory, but Epoch lacks a training-compute estimate. Supplying our derived FLOP per SCHEMA note. The 5.6T figure is the full multimodal corpus; text-only active-param FLOP is the floor here.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 47e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.58e24 FLOP. Text-only MoE variant, 300B total / 47B active, same 5.6T corpus per the ERNIE 4.5 technical report.
notes: Text-only flagship (distinct from the 424B VL model already in Epoch). Same active-param count so similar FLOP; lower confidence because the 300B variant's exact token schedule may differ from the VL model's.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 3e9 active params x 5.6e12 tokens = 1.01e23 FLOP. 21B total / 3B active MoE per official release; assumes same 5.6T corpus as the family (uncertain for the small variant).
notes: Compact MoE. Token count assumed equal to family corpus (5.6T); if the small model was trained on fewer tokens, this overstates. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: ~1T total MoE; active params undisclosed. Assuming ~10% active (~100B) x ~10e12 tokens x 6 = 6e24 FLOP. Both active-param fraction and token count are guesses; trillion-param total is the only firm figure.
notes: Released at WAIC July 2024 (date approximate). Trillion-param MoE; StepFun disclosed neither active params nor tokens, so this is a pure estimate. Was China's top-ranked LLM on LiveBench at release. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 38e9 active params x 20e12 tokens = 4.56e24 FLOP. 321B total / 38B active MoE, trained on 20T+ tokens (incl. 4T multimodal) per StepFun's release and technical report (arXiv 2507.19427).
notes: Open-source multimodal reasoning VLM. Active-param and token counts are official; FLOP is our 6ND derivation. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 11e9 active params x 17.2e12 tokens = 1.14e24 FLOP. 196B total / 11B active MoE, 17.2T tokens per StepFun (arXiv 2602.10604). Date is month-only (YYYY-MM-01).
notes: Active params and tokens official; FLOP is our 6ND derivation. A Step-3.7-Flash (May 2026) also exists with fewer disclosed details. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 7e9 dense params x 2.6e12 tokens = 1.09e23 FLOP. Both figures from the Baichuan 2 technical report (arXiv 2309.10305); dense model, 2.6T tokens.
notes: Well-grounded: both params and tokens are officially reported. Standard 6ND dense estimate. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 13e9 dense params x 2.6e12 tokens = 2.03e23 FLOP. Both figures from the Baichuan 2 technical report (arXiv 2309.10305); dense model, 2.6T tokens.
notes: Well-grounded: both params and tokens officially reported. Standard 6ND dense estimate. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: reported as >100B (some coverage says ~1T) params; architecture undisclosed. Assuming a dense-equivalent ~200B trained on ~2.5T tokens x 6 = 3e24. Both params and tokens are guesses.
notes: Closed model; conflicting param reports (>100B vs ~1T). No official tokens or compute. Pure estimate, flagged low confidence. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: No official parameter count, token count, or compute disclosed. Closed model. No defensible FLOP estimate possible beyond noting it is a generational successor to Baichuan 3 (likely similar or larger scale, low-single-digit e24).
notes: Closed flagship, ranked #1 on SuperCLUE in China at release. No specs disclosed; FLOP left null. Included for completeness/dating. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Estimated: 6 x 14e9 dense params x 20e12 tokens = 1.68e24 FLOP. Both figures official: 14B dense, trained from scratch on 20T tokens per the Baichuan-M1 technical report (arXiv 2502.12671).
notes: Medical-specialized LLM trained from scratch. Well-grounded: params and tokens both officially reported. High token-to-param ratio. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: 600B-param MoE trained on >10TB of tokens. Assuming ~10TB ~= ~4T tokens and treating active params as ~100B (MoE) x 4e12 x 6 = 2.4e24; but if dense-equivalent at 600B x 4T x 6 = 1.4e25. Reported as MoE, so true figure is likely below the dense bound. Using the dense bound as an upper estimate.
notes: Closed MoE; '10TB tokens' is a data-volume claim not a token count (TB->token conversion is rough, ~2.5-4 chars/token). Active params undisclosed, so 6ND is highly uncertain. Estimate flagged low confidence. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: incremental update over SenseNova 5.0 (~30% performance gain), same >10TB token claim and ~600B MoE. Assuming modestly more compute than 5.0; same dense-equivalent upper bound ~1.5e25. Active params undisclosed.
notes: Closed MoE update of 5.0. Same caveats as SenseNova 5.0 (TB-not-tokens, undisclosed active params). Low confidence. Not in Epoch inventory. (SenseNova V6, Apr 2025, >600B MoE, also exists but with no token disclosure.)

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: trillion-param-class model trained on Huawei Ascend ('Feixing-1' cluster). No params/tokens/compute disclosed. Assuming a dense-equivalent ~few-hundred-B x ~few-T tokens, ~2e24 order. Pure guess.
notes: Closed model, trained entirely on Huawei Ascend (notable: domestic-chip training). No disclosed scale; FLOP is a rough order-of-magnitude guess. Date approximate. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Reasoning model trained entirely on Huawei Ascend 910B. No params, tokens, or compute disclosed. iFlytek emphasizes compute-efficiency ('top results with less computing power'), so scale is unknown. No defensible FLOP estimate.
notes: Deep-reasoning model, fully Ascend-trained (Ascend 910B), upgraded April 2025. Notable as a near-entirely domestic-chip training run. No scale disclosed; FLOP null. Not in Epoch inventory.

compute_basis: Back-of-envelope: ~1T total MoE; active params undisclosed. Assuming ~8% active (~80B) x ~8e12 tokens x 6 = 3.8e24. Both active fraction and tokens are guesses; only trillion-param total is stated.
notes: Trillion-param MoE, 200K context. Predecessor abab6 (Jan 2024) was China's first >100B MoE LLM. No active-param or token disclosure, so pure estimate. Not in Epoch inventory.
